name: Warm Season Fungicide Rd 1
talk: Grass is coming out of dormancy — weakened turf is vulnerable to disease during green-up. This prevents fungus before it starts and includes nutrition.
yard: Green-up phase. Weaker grass susceptible to disease.

name: Warm Season Fungicide Rd 2
talk: Peak heat and moisture — prime conditions for disease. We rotate to a different fungicide chemistry to prevent resistance. Higher fertilizer rate this round.
yard: Fully growing. Heat stress and moisture create disease risk.

name: Warm Season Fungicide Rd 3
talk: Heading into dormancy. Moisture doesn't absorb as fast, creating fungal risk with cooler temps. Last preventative round of the season.
yard: Going dormant. Slower moisture absorption = fungal risk.

name: Fescue Fungicide Rd 1
talk: Starting preventative disease program before summer stress hits fescue. Includes nutrition to support the grass through the transition.

name: Fescue Fungicide Rd 2
talk: Rotated to a strobilurin fungicide (different mode of action). Earth Max supports soil health during increasing heat.
yard: Heat and humidity increasing. Disease pressure building.

name: Fescue Fungicide Rd 3
talk: Continued prevention through the highest-risk period. Heat and humidity are the biggest drivers of fescue disease. Staying on schedule is critical.

name: Fescue Fungicide Rd 4
talk: Peak summer stress — fescue is most vulnerable right now. Missing this round is when we see the biggest disease losses.

name: Fescue Fungicide Rd 5
talk: Final preventative round before fall recovery. Rotated back to Propiconazole. Heat is breaking and fescue will start bouncing back.
yard: Heat breaking. Transition to fall recovery.
